sources.label: Reece Group investor centre (no new ASX announcements surfaced since Jun 6)
Market Index - REH share price and broker coverage

verdicts.rankRationale: No new evidence since June 6 that Reece's plumbing/HVAC distribution network is actually aggregating water-intensive compute-site demand; a +2.5% day and +6.75% YTD is benign drift, not a re-rating that erodes residual alpha, so the rank-96 strict-screen placement stands as per yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: Reece's US branch roll-out and dominant ANZ plumbing/waterworks footprint position it as a quiet local aggregator if water-cooled data center builds accelerate, and at ~$6B cap it remains entirely un-owned as an AI name.
verdicts.neutral: It is a housing-cycle distributor first; FY26 EBIT guidance of A$520-540m already reflects cost inflation, and any compute-site revenue is currently too small to surface in disclosures.
verdicts.bear: The AI-bottleneck pathway is speculative with zero disclosed data-center contract evidence, the stock trades at a premium multiple (~30x), and sell-side is split (Goldman at Sell vs Morgan Stanley Overweight), leaving it exposed to a housing downcycle with no AI offset.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: FY26 results on Aug 24, 2026, or any first disclosed waterworks/cooling supply win tied to a data center campus.
verdicts.primaryRisk: US and ANZ residential construction softness compresses margins while the compute-site thesis remains unevidenced, leaving a richly valued cyclical.
